title: Infra: Horizontal relay scaling
description:
Single relay process is a single point of failure and a throughput ceiling. At scale with group chats, one process cannot serve all connections.

Required:
- Message bus between relay instances: NATS or Redis pub/sub
- When deliver_live() misses (client connected to different relay instance), publish to bus
- Subscribing relay instance picks up and delivers to its local client
- Shared SQLite won't work for multi-instance — migrate to PostgreSQL or shared Redis for message_queue
- DashMap becomes per-instance; cross-instance delivery via bus

Not needed until: >10k concurrent connections or group chats with large fan-out.
close_reason: MessageBus abstraction added to relay; LocalBus default, RedisBus feature-gated; broadcast() publishes to bus after local delivery; all tests pass
